purpose: Inventory candidate artifacts and finite predicates for the missing K900-to-chamber/register map proof.
boundary: This ledger does not prove the chamber/register map.
This ledger does not change the Perfect Return Cell UI.
This ledger does not claim that upstairs/downstairs is a finite invariant.
This ledger only records available sources, candidate input sets, controls, and next proof scripts.

target_theorem_A0.statement: A finite search over candidate register keys either produces a lawful register map that recovers the selected WXYZTIW branch triple, or proves that public predicates are insufficient and row-aware incidence is the first tested exact layer.

candidate_input_sets.purpose: Test whether public chamber/triangle predicates alone recover the WXYZTIW selected branch triple.
candidate_input_sets.expected_result: Likely reduces candidate space but does not recover exact branch law.

candidate_input_sets.purpose: Test whether row-aware M-incidence is the first layer that exactly recovers the selected branch triple.
candidate_input_sets.expected_result: Exact recovery if v21 conclusion is reproduced.

candidate_input_sets.purpose: Control against accidental recovery by the shape of M alone.
Test whether public chamber/triangle predicates reduce the register space to Petersen mate-pairs before row-aware incidence resolves the branch law.
candidate_input_sets.expected_result: Should identify partial key reduction, not full selection.

summary.preview:
# FHE result v21

Status: Exact selector minimizer checkpoint.

## Question

After v20 found that row-aware M-incidence exactly separates the observed selected-branch triples from rejected abstract candidates, what is the least-sharp exact selector in the tested feature family?

This matters because support_row_degree_profile is exact, but it has one distinct value per local candidate row:

    values = 210

So it may be close to key material.

## Result

The full minimizer scanned all tested feature combinations:

    combo_count_scanned = 2047
    exact_combo_count = 1384
    one_feature_exact_count = 1

The only one-feature exact selector is:

    support_row_degree_profile

with:

    values = 210

Thus support_row_degree_profile is the minimal single-feature exact selector in the tested family.

## Least-sharp exact tested selectors

The least-value exact selectors have:

    values = 206
    feature_count = 3

The first least-sharp exact selectors are:

    block_word
    support_intersection_size
    support_complement

and the equivalent union/complement variants:

    block_word
    support_intersection_size
    support_union

    block_word
    support_row_degree_hist

\section{Future companion direction: quotient visibility and transport residue}

A future companion result may record that quotient-visible coherence does not imply
complete transport agreement.

In finite orientation-closure examples, two lifted sign systems may share the
same quotient-visible graph and edge partition while differing in closed-walk
orientation residue. Thus a trace space recording quotient incidence alone may
be insufficient to witness transport closure.

This companion result is not required for the basic three-stage separation,
but it strengthens the motivation for choosing trace spaces carefully.

# Selected branch exact selector minimizer checkpoint
Recompute all exact row-aware selector combinations and identify the least-sharp exact selectors.
The branch anatomy audit asks which zero-sign G15 carrier edges can serve as the \(ZT\) central coupler in the role-relative pattern
There are fifteen zero-sign carrier edges. Exactly three satisfy the top-lock \(ZT\) role test:
Each selected edge is an even-triangle edge whose endpoints are both odd-active. This gives the current branch-anatomy keeper line:
This establishes that the three branches are not arbitrary representatives of a shared aggregate trace. They are selected by the K900-WXYZTIW role-relative closure test.

The K900-native collision census in Section~\ref{sec:k900-native-collision-bound} gives the Track A result: within the K900 carrier, the target predicate selects exactly three base cycles and six oriented presentations.

The selected branch triple is an edge-level object in this register. It does not live on the fixed triangle itself. For every public keypair, the selected triple lies in the complement of the fixed Petersen-star triangle

Equivalently, the selected branch triple is a three-edge matching in \(G_{15}\). It uses six distinct \(G_{15}\) vertices arranged across exactly three \(\tau\)-swapped vertex-pairs: two pairs are crosslinked by the move

Exact recovery in the tested feature family requires row-aware incidence. The row-aware support-degree profile of the sector matrix \(M\), recording which rows are touched and with what multiplicity, separates all \(60\)

As a single feature, this row-aware profile is exact but sharp: it has \(210\) distinct values on the \(210\) local candidate rows. A minimizer over the tested feature family found that the only one-feature exact selecto

The current checkpoint isolates a provisional K900-locked WXYZTIW class, denoted \(A_{\mathrm{pre}}\), consisting of three concrete six-cycle witnesses:

These witnesses share a common \(W\)-\(X\)-\(Y\) stem and \(I\)-\(W\) return, while differing only in the central \(Z\)-\(T\) branch. A coarse aggregate observable preserves the three-way class but fails balanced two-fli

A subsequent branch anatomy audit shows that among fifteen zero-sign carrier edges, exactly three can serve as the top-lock \(Z T\) coupler: \(3\!-\!4\), \(7\!-\!10\), and \(8\!-\!14\). Each is an even-triangle edge with

A second finite audit identifies a register-key layer: public chamber/triangle predicates reduce the register space to Petersen mate-pairs, while the selected branch law is recovered exactly only at the row-aware incide
The current FHE branch establishes a finite local projection-separation checkpoint inside the imported K900 G15 carrier.
\item a three-way K900-WXYZTIW projection is isolated;
\item the selected branches are exactly the zero-sign even couplers between odd-active sides satisfying the top-lock role pattern;
\item role-relative trace preserves the three-way projection while rejecting the tested one-edge and two-edge sign tampers;

The later register-key audits add a bounded explanatory layer to the K900-native collision result. Public chamber/triangle predicates reduce the register space to structured Petersen mate-pairs rather than a unique key.
